Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Personal Encyclopedias 610 upvotes, 127 comments
  2. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 41 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Obsolete Sounds 147 upvotes, 28 comments
  4. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 71 upvotes, 11 comments
  5. European Parliament decided that Chat Control 1.0 must stop 472 upvotes, 114 comments
  6. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1361 upvotes, 367 comments
  7. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 39 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Social-Media Addiction Trial 45 upvotes, 7 comments
  9. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 195 upvotes, 53 comments
  10. End of "Chat Control": EU Parliament Stops Mass Surveillance in Voting Thriller 266 upvotes, 77 comments
  11. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 767 upvotes, 258 comments
  12. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 203 upvotes, 60 comments
  13. Cory Doctorow: Interoperability Can Save the Open Web 81 upvotes, 20 comments
  14. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 200 upvotes, 69 comments
  15. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 343 upvotes, 158 comments
  16. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 239 upvotes, 116 comments
  17. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 42 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  19. French e, è, é, ê, ë – what's the difference? 37 upvotes, 14 comments
  20. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 193 upvotes, 106 comments
  21. Niche Museums 69 upvotes, 34 comments
  22. ARC-AGI-3 464 upvotes, 298 comments
  23. Swift 6.3 241 upvotes, 148 comments
  24. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 161 upvotes, 98 comments
  25. Landmark L.A. jury verdict finds Instagram, YouTube were designed to addict kids 259 upvotes, 184 comments
  26. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 223 upvotes, 189 comments
  27. What came after the 486? 118 upvotes, 94 comments
  28. Newly purchased Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts to use smart features 114 upvotes, 111 comments
  29. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 8 upvotes, 3 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:30am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
